---
title: "NEXER REVEALJS TEMPLATE"
subtitle: "Quarto presentation starter"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  nexer-revealjs: default
---

## A template is only useful when it moves quickly

This starter deck is designed for sharp internal and client-facing presentations.

- Use uppercase messaging for the strongest moments
- Keep layouts spacious and fast to scan
- Let accent color signal emphasis, not decorate everything

::: footer
A reusable Quarto RevealJS presentation for Nexer
:::

## POINT OF VIEW {.section-slide}

## BUILT FOR CLEAR NARRATIVE, NOT DECORATIVE NOISE

::: {.lede}
A Nexer slide should feel precise, modern, and intentional.
:::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
### What the system favors

- Strong headline rhythm
- Short supporting copy
- High-contrast information design
- Controlled accent color
- Slides that survive both room presentation and PDF export
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}
::: {.metric-card}
<span class="metric-value">16:9</span>
<span class="metric-label">Default widescreen frame</span>
:::
:::
::::

## TWO-SPEED COMMUNICATION

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}
### Message first

Your opening line should carry the argument. The rest of the slide should only support it.
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
### Detail second

Use lists, comparison blocks, and speaker notes to add specificity without crowding the frame.
:::
::::

## SIGNAL EMPHASIS WITHOUT LOSING RESTRAINT

::: {.statement}
Most slides should stay black, white, and grey.
:::

::: {.accent-panel}
Use orange or warm red sparingly when a slide needs urgency, contrast, or a single focal point.
:::

## QUOTE OR POSITIONING SLIDE {.quote-slide}

> WE HELP ORGANIZATIONS MOVE FROM AMBITION TO EXECUTION WITHOUT LOSING CLARITY.

::: {.attribution}
Nexer presentation system
:::

## OPTIONAL VISUAL MOMENT {background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #190878 0%, #5a1ea0 52%, #aa4bf5 100%)"}

::: {.inverse-block}
**Use this kind of slide sparingly**

One strong visual interruption can reset attention and create a section break without turning the whole deck into a keynote imitation.
:::

## IMAGE-LED STORYTELLING WITHOUT CLUTTER

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
::: {.image-stage}
![](assets/images/Women office iPad 3.jpeg){fig-alt="Colleagues collaborating around a tablet"}

::: {.image-stage__caption}
**Use photography with discipline**

Pick one image with enough negative space for message hierarchy and keep overlays short.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### What makes the image work

- Human focus without feeling like stock filler
- Space for a short argument on top
- Neutral enough to fit many team stories
- Strong contrast for black, white, and accent treatments
:::
::::

## CONTENT SLIDE WITH FOOTNOTES AND NOTES

### Delivery checklist

- Keep each slide to one idea
- Prefer three strong bullets over seven weak ones
- Use the symbol as a complement, not a substitute for the logo^[Keep the symbol as a supporting brand marker rather than a replacement for the wordmark.]

::: aside
This template keeps the demo mostly image-neutral so it remains reusable for many teams.
:::

::: {.notes}
Use speaker notes for examples, client specifics, or transition language.
:::

## CLOSING SLIDE {footer=false}

::: {.closing-mark}
![](_extensions/nexer/nexer-symbol-black.svg){fig-alt="Nexer symbol"}
:::

### READY TO ADAPT

Swap the message, keep the structure, and render.
